name: Toll House Layercake
recipeIngredient: 1 package (12 oz) Chocolate chips, "Little Bits" semi-sweet, divided
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 15-1/2 x 10-1/2 x 1-inch baking pan and line with waxed paper.
In a large bowl, cream the butter and brown sugar until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in the vanilla and salt.
Gradually add the flour to the butter mixture, alternating with the chocolate bits. Stir until just combined.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow the cake to cool completely before removing from the pan.
Once cooled, loosen the sides of the cake and invert onto a floured towel. Peel off the waxed paper.
Cut the cake crosswise into 4 sections.
For frosting: Melt 1 cup of chocolate bits over hot water until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, beat the remaining butter until creamy. Gradually add the confectioners' sugar and stir in the melted chocolate and vanilla. Blend until smooth.
Assemble the cake by spreading frosting on one layer, placing the second layer on top, and repeating with the remaining layers. Frost the top and sides of the cake.
